tests.description: handle registered as a match-arm statement fires on the handled interrupt raised later in the same arm
An interrupt the handler propagates (approved externally, pausing/resuming the node) sits between handle registration and the handled interrupt; the handler still fires after resume
A mid-arm return inside an if yields the match before the handle statement is reached, so the handler never registers and never fires
Control: when the early-return branch is not taken, the handle statement IS reached and the handler fires normally
